marketTitle: U.K. Composite Utility Transmission Pole Market - Customer Database
subtitle: Verified directory and insight on customers
entityNote: (Entity Across Transmission System Operators and Transmission Utilities, Distribution Network Operators, EPC and Grid Infrastructure Contractors, Industrial Power Network Owners, Government and Public Transmission Agencies)

proposition1.label: Proposition 1 - Basic
titleLines: U.K. Composite Utility Transmission Pole Market - Customer Database
Verified directory and insight on customers
(Entity Across Electric Transmission Utilities, Transmission System Operators, EPC and Grid Infrastructure Contractors, Industrial Power Network Owners, Government and Public Transmission Agencies)

rows.businessOverview: Electricity distribution network operator maintaining electricity networks across London, the South East, and the East of England. It operates through Eastern Power Networks, London Power Networks, and South Eastern Power Networks.
rows.compositePoleApplicationUseCase: Engineered / composite-alternative pole application for 33 kilovolt distribution overhead-line networks. Public evidence supports UK Power Networks’ engineered pole trial on a 33 kilovolt line.
rows.annualRevenueOrNetworkBudgetSignal: FY2025 turnover: £2,462.9 million, approximately US$3,100 million; strong regulated network capital expenditure signal.
rows.sizeOperatingScale: Major electricity distribution network covering London, South East England, and East of England; large urban, semi-urban, and rural distribution network base.
rows.designationDecisionMakerRole: Director of Capital Programme and Procurement

rows.businessOverview: Electricity transmission and distribution network operator under ScottishPower, responsible for electricity networks across Central and Southern Scotland, Merseyside, Cheshire, North Wales, and North Shropshire.
rows.compositePoleApplicationUseCase: Potential composite pole use for overhead line reinforcement, wood pole replacement, storm resilience, and renewable grid connection projects.
rows.annualRevenueOrNetworkBudgetSignal: Strong network-investment signal through RIIO-ED2 and RIIO-T3 regulated grid investment programmes.
rows.sizeOperatingScale: Large U.K. electricity network operator with both transmission and distribution responsibilities.
rows.keyContactPerson:
Mhairi McGilp
NEIL HARKINS (Alternate Person to Contact)
rows.designationDecisionMakerRole:
Category Manager
Head of Energy Networks Purchasing

rows.customerCompanyName: SSEN Transmission (Scottish and Southern Electricity Networks)
rows.businessOverview: Electricity transmission network owner and operator responsible for the north of Scotland transmission network and renewable energy grid connections.
rows.customerTypeAndNetworkResponsibility: Transmission System Operator, Transmission Utility and Distribution Network Operator Group
rows.compositePoleApplicationUseCase: Used composite poles for Dorenell Wind Farm transmission connection project and remote-terrain grid infrastructure deployment.
rows.annualRevenueOrNetworkBudgetSignal: Multi-billion-pound RIIO-T3 transmission investment plan; projected network investment above US$ 25 billion equivalent over 2026–2031.
rows.sizeOperatingScale: Large-scale U.K. transmission network operator covering north of Scotland transmission infrastructure.
rows.designationDecisionMakerRole: Director of Procurement at SSEN Transmission

rows.businessOverview: Electricity distribution network operator maintaining electricity networks across London, the South East, and the East of England. It operates through Eastern Power Networks, London Power Networks, and South Eastern Power Networks.
rows.compositePoleApplicationUseCase: Engineered / composite-alternative pole application for 33 kilovolt distribution overhead-line networks. Public evidence supports UK Power Networks’ engineered pole trial on a 33 kilovolt line.
rows.annualRevenueOrNetworkBudgetSignal: FY2025 turnover: £2,462.9 million, approximately US$3,100 million; strong regulated network capital expenditure signal.
rows.sizeOperatingScale: Major electricity distribution network covering London, South East England, and East of England; large urban, semi-urban, and rural distribution network base.
rows.designationDecisionMakerRole: Director of Capital Programme and Procurement
rows.compositePolePurchaseCriteria: Engineering approval, 33 kilovolt network suitability, installation safety, lifecycle cost, low maintenance, outage reduction, compatibility with existing overhead-line assets
rows.networkPainPoints: Aging overhead-line assets, storm resilience, restricted access, urban and semi-rural disruption, outage minimisation, rising network load from electrification
rows.gridComplianceAndOperationalIssues: Distribution network safety standards, engineering specifications, asset-management approval, environmental and wayleave constraints, Distribution Network Operator regulatory obligations
rows.riskExposure: Service interruption risk, public safety risk, project delay risk, asset defect risk, regulatory performance risk
rows.assetRenewalCycleAndBuyingTriggers: 33 kilovolt overhead-line upgrade, engineered pole trial, asset replacement cycle, resilience projects, electric vehicle and distributed energy connection growth
rows.budgetOwner: Asset Management, Network Engineering, Capital Delivery, Procurement; Information Technology may influence asset data, digital inspection, and network analytics systems
rows.procurementModel: Technical trial, engineering specification, approved supplier qualification, framework procurement, project call-off
rows.vendorSelectionCriteria: Proven distribution-network performance, compliance documentation, constructability, crew safety, installation procedure, warranty, United Kingdom references
rows.preferredEngagementMode: Technical workshop, engineering evaluation, pilot deployment, approved supplier onboarding
rows.preferredDeploymentServiceContract: Project-based deployment with potential for framework supply after successful trial and business-as-usual adoption
rows.preferredSolutionType: Engineered / composite distribution pole solution for 33 kilovolt overhead-line applications
rows.integrationTechnicalServiceRequirement: Structural testing, pole-top hardware compatibility, foundation design, installation procedure, asset data integration, field crew training
rows.potentialCustomerDetails: UK Power Networks is a strong Distribution Network Operator prospect because it has already evaluated engineered poles through a 33 kilovolt overhead-line innovation project. Andrew Quail can be treated as a digital / information systems contact, while the actual pole buyer should be mapped under Asset Management, Network Engineering, Procurement, or Capital Delivery.
rows.additionalCmiNotes: Good prospect for composite / engineered pole suppliers, but classify carefully: evidence supports distribution-network engineered pole trial. Priority is medium-high due to network scale, engineering maturity, and potential for asset-standard adoption.
